name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 18.05.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 18.05.2026.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 18.05.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 18.05.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on May 15, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 83.08 lakh ha as compared to 80.01 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 31.05 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year. There is also an increase in the area sown under Maize by about 1.50 lakh ha so far from the last 5-years average.
texts.text:  As on May 15, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 83.08 lakh ha as compared to 80.01 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 31.05 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year. There is also an increase in the area sown under Maize by about 1.50 lakh ha so far from the last 5-years average.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 15.05.2026, 96.86% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Pulses harvest has been completed; 99.87% area of Oilseeds- majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (100%); 98.62% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals- majorly in Jowar (99.66%), 72.70% area of Rice and 98.80% area of Wheat has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 15.05.2026, 96.86% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Pulses harvest has been completed; 99.87% area of Oilseeds- majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (100%); 98.62% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals- majorly in Jowar (99.66%), 72.70% area of Rice and 98.80% area of Wheat has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  North  West  India, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, northern part of Coastal Andhra Pradesh, East India and many parts of North East India.
texts.text: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  North  West  India, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, northern part of Coastal Andhra Pradesh, East India and many parts of North East India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  17  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  4% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 10%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 4%; (iii) Central India: 30%; and South Peninsula: (-) 12%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  17  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  4% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 10%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 4%; (iii) Central India: 30%; and South Peninsula: (-) 12%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  also  likely  over  northeast  India  and  over  Andaman  & Nicobar Islands, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim during next 6-7 days; and over Tamil Nadu,  Puducherry  &  Karaikal,  Kerala  &  Mahe,  Lakshadweep  and  South  Interior  Karnataka during next 3 days.
texts.text:  Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  also  likely  over  northeast  India  and  over  Andaman  & Nicobar Islands, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim during next 6-7 days; and over Tamil Nadu,  Puducherry  &  Karaikal,  Kerala  &  Mahe,  Lakshadweep  and  South  Interior  Karnataka during next 3 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Scattered  to  Isolated  light  to  moderate  rainfall  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty  winds likely over Jammu-Kashmir on 19th & 20th; Uttarakhand during 20th -23rd May.
texts.text:  Scattered  to  Isolated  light  to  moderate  rainfall  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty  winds likely over Jammu-Kashmir on 19th & 20th; Uttarakhand during 20th -23rd May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  light  to  moderate  rainfall  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty  winds  likely  over Chhattisgarh  and  Madhya Maharashtra during 18 th May  and  Himachal  Pradesh  during 19th  23rd May.
texts.text:  Isolated  light  to  moderate  rainfall  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty  winds  likely  over Chhattisgarh  and  Madhya Maharashtra during 18 th May  and  Himachal  Pradesh  during 19th  23rd May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat wave to severe heat wave conditions likely to prevail over plains of northwest India and central India during many days of the week.
texts.text:  Heat wave to severe heat wave conditions likely to prevail over plains of northwest India and central India during many days of the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  14  May  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 63.232 billion cubic meter, which is 34.45% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 112.91 percent of last  year's  storage  and  123.86  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  14  May  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 63.232 billion cubic meter, which is 34.45% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 112.91 percent of last  year's  storage  and  123.86  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic Threshold Level for Litchi (Stinkbug and Mealy bug) in Bihar and for Rice (Neck Blast) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic Threshold Level for Litchi (Stinkbug and Mealy bug) in Bihar and for Rice (Neck Blast) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (07 May - 13 May 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 21%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 13 May 2026) is higher than LPA by 4%.
texts.text:  During the week (07 May - 13 May 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 21%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 13 May 2026) is higher than LPA by 4%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall,  thunderstorm  activity  is  likely  to  continue  over  the  east  &  northeast,  and  the  southern peninsular India during many days of the week.
texts.text: Overall,  thunderstorm  activity  is  likely  to  continue  over  the  east  &  northeast,  and  the  southern peninsular India during many days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall is likely to be above normal over the Central, South Peninsular India and over East & Northeast India; below normal over the Western Himalayan region and plains of northwest India during the week.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely to be above normal over the Central, South Peninsular India and over East & Northeast India; below normal over the Western Himalayan region and plains of northwest India during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (15  May - 21 May 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (15  May - 21 May 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 3-5°C likely over many parts of Northwest India till 21th May and no significant change during 22nd -23rd May.
texts.text:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 3-5°C likely over many parts of Northwest India till 21th May and no significant change during 22nd -23rd May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Maharashtra and Gujarat State till 19th and gradual fall by 2-3°C during 20th -22nd May.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Maharashtra and Gujarat State till 19th and gradual fall by 2-3°C during 20th -22nd May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual  rise  in  maximum  temperatures  by  2-3°C  likely  over  parts  of  East,  Northeast  India  and Telangana till 19th May and no significant change during 20th1 to 23rd May.
texts.text:  Gradual  rise  in  maximum  temperatures  by  2-3°C  likely  over  parts  of  East,  Northeast  India  and Telangana till 19th May and no significant change during 20th1 to 23rd May.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over rest parts of the country till 23nd May 2026.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over rest parts of the country till 23nd May 2026.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (22  May - 28 May 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (22  May - 28 May 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low to moderate probability of Heatwave conditions likely over many parts of northwest, west and central India during many days of the week.
texts.text:  Low to moderate probability of Heatwave conditions likely over many parts of northwest, west and central India during many days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Appreciably  above  normal  (+3.1 to  +5.0°C)  maximum  temperatures  are  likely  over  many parts  of northwest India; above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) temperatures are likely along the northwest, west and central India and over some parts of northeast India. Below-normal maximum temperatures (-1.6 to 3.0°C)  are  likely  across  many  parts  of  South  Peninsular  India,  and  Near-normal  maximum temperatures are likely across the rest of the country during the week.
texts.text:  Appreciably  above  normal  (+3.1 to  +5.0°C)  maximum  temperatures  are  likely  over  many parts  of northwest India; above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) temperatures are likely along the northwest, west and central India and over some parts of northeast India. Below-normal maximum temperatures (-1.6 to 3.0°C)  are  likely  across  many  parts  of  South  Peninsular  India,  and  Near-normal  maximum temperatures are likely across the rest of the country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low probability of warm night conditions is likely over Punjab, Haryana, and adjoining West Uttar Pradesh on some days of the week.
texts.text:  Low probability of warm night conditions is likely over Punjab, Haryana, and adjoining West Uttar Pradesh on some days of the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:
texts.text: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Southwest monsoon has further advanced into some more parts of southeast Arabian Sea, some parts of Comorin area, some more parts of southwest and southeast Bay of Bengal, most parts of Andaman Sea, entire Andaman & Nicobar Islands and some parts of eastcentral Bay of Bengal today, the 18th May 2026.
texts.text:  Southwest monsoon has further advanced into some more parts of southeast Arabian Sea, some parts of Comorin area, some more parts of southwest and southeast Bay of Bengal, most parts of Andaman Sea, entire Andaman & Nicobar Islands and some parts of eastcentral Bay of Bengal today, the 18th May 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of southeast Arabian Sea, Comorin Area, southwest and southeast Bay of Bengal, remaining parts of Andaman Sea, and some more parts of eastcentral Bay of Bengal during next 3-4 days.
texts.text:  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of southeast Arabian Sea, Comorin Area, southwest and southeast Bay of Bengal, remaining parts of Andaman Sea, and some more parts of eastcentral Bay of Bengal during next 3-4 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Southwest Monsoon is likely to set in over Kerala on 26th May with a model error of ± 4 days.
texts.text:  The Southwest Monsoon is likely to set in over Kerala on 26th May with a model error of ± 4 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. CROPS
texts.text: 2. CROPS

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rabi Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Rabi Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rice : About 31.05 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice : About 31.05 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses : About 24.97 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 23.76 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses : About 24.97 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 23.76 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Oilseeds : About 11.04 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 9.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Oilseeds : About 11.04 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 9.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Source: Crops Division, DoA&FW.
texts.text: Source: Crops Division, DoA&FW.

texts.orig: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE
texts.text: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As on 14 May, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 63.232 BCM (34.45%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 14 May, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 63.232 BCM (34.45%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  135 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.
texts.text:  135 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.

texts.orig:  18 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.
texts.text:  18 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.

texts.text:  13 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.
